title: StandardAssociation data model + vocabulary-mapping table (standard↔standard crosswalk layer)
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed alignment records linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID primary / ASN URI optional), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. PLAN.md v0.2 adds a second record type — the StandardAssociation (standard↔standard crosswalk layer) — that links a standard in one framework to a related, equivalent, or superseding standard in another, under the same dual gate and review. A vocabulary-mapping table (our type/associationType → schema.org alignmentType → CASE CFAssociationType) is also required so the four export formats never diverge semantically. Both are M0 exit criteria #1 in the v0.2 plan.
objective: Create the canonical StandardAssociation data model, authoring template, and vocabulary-mapping table that the standard↔standard crosswalk layer and all exporters will use.
acceptanceCriteria: Canonical StandardAssociation documents all fields: from {frameworkId, statementId, caseGuid, asnUri, frameworkTerms {license, permitsReference, permitsTextReproduction, ref}}, to {frameworkId, statementId, caseGuid, asnUri, frameworkTerms {...}}, associationType (exactMatch | isEquivalentTo | isRelatedTo | isPartOf | supersedes), strength, coverage, evidence, aiConfidence, review {status, reviewedBy, reviewerQualification, reviewedAt, auditedBy}, notEndorsement (always true — a reviewed proposal, never authoritative equivalence), outputLicense.

Vocabulary-mapping table published and treated as the single source of truth for all exporters: maps our type/associationType to schema.org alignmentType and CASE CFAssociationType v1.1, confirmed against the pinned CASE v1.1 vocabulary so the four export formats (CSV, JSON, schema.org, CASE) never diverge semantically.

Template states that every StandardAssociation is an expert-reviewed proposal carrying notEndorsement:true — never an assertion of official equivalence — and that CASE GUID is the primary identifier required where one exists.

strength, coverage, aiConfidence, reviewerQualification, and notEndorsement carry into every export (CASE/schema.org/CSV projections), not only the dataset-level disclaimer, so a downstream consumer cannot silently render a proposal as authoritative fact.

At least one fully worked example StandardAssociation is included (e.g. CCSS↔a state framework or Australian Curriculum↔England NC for one subject/grade band); outputLicense: CC-BY-4.0; commit is DCO signed-off.
resources: PLAN.md (v0.2 — StandardAssociation record type, vocabulary-mapping table, key decisions)
